repaired_examples.Show A Clean Pair Of Heels: The thief showed a clean pair of heels the moment the alarm rang.
repaired_examples.For All Intents And Purposes: For all intents and purposes, the project was finished by Friday.
repaired_examples.Paled Into Insignificance: His earlier troubles paled into insignificance beside this loss.
repaired_examples.Old Head On Young Shoulders: At just nineteen, she has an old head on young shoulders.
repaired_examples.Leave No Stone Unturned: The police left no stone unturned in tracing the missing boy.
repaired_examples.Too Many Chiefs And Not Enough Indians: The committee failed because there were too many chiefs and not enough Indians.
repaired_examples.Keep Body And Soul Together: The daily wager earned barely enough to keep body and soul together.
repaired_examples.Wrangle Over An Ass's Shadow: The two clerks wrangled over an ass's shadow for an entire afternoon.
repaired_examples.To Put One's Hand To The Plough: Once he put his hand to the plough, he never looked back.
repaired_examples.Between The Horns Of A Dilemma: She was between the horns of a dilemma over which offer to accept.
repaired_examples.Square Peg In A Round Hole: As an accountant, the artist felt like a square peg in a round hole.
repaired_examples.Let The Chips Fall Where They May: He told the truth and let the chips fall where they may.
repaired_examples.To Kill Two Birds With One Stone: By cycling to college, he killed two birds with one stone — saving money and staying fit.
repaired_examples.Wear Your Heart On Your Sleeve: He wears his heart on his sleeve and hides nothing from his friends.
repaired_examples.Actions Speak Louder Than Words: He promised to help, but actions speak louder than words.
repaired_examples.Practice Makes A Man Perfect: Solve a paper daily; practice makes a man perfect.
repaired_examples.To Bite Off More Than One Can Chew: Taking five optional subjects, he bit off more than he could chew.
repaired_examples.To Make A Long Story Short: To make a long story short, the deal finally fell through.
repaired_examples.To Steal Someone's Thunder: She stole his thunder by announcing the result before he could.
repaired_examples.Rome Was Not Built In A Day: Be patient with your preparation; Rome was not built in a day.
repaired_examples.Familiarity Breeds Contempt: They quarrelled often after sharing a room, proving familiarity breeds contempt.
repaired_examples.Don't Put All Your Eggs In One Basket: Apply for several exams; don't put all your eggs in one basket.
repaired_examples.To Put The Cat Among The Pigeons: His blunt question put the cat among the pigeons at the meeting.
repaired_examples.Beauty Is In The Eye Of The Beholder: Some found the painting odd, but beauty is in the eye of the beholder.
repaired_examples.Necessity Is The Mother Of Invention: The villagers built a bamboo bridge — necessity is the mother of invention.
repaired_examples.Discretion Is The Better Part Of Valour: He withdrew from the argument, knowing discretion is the better part of valour.
repaired_examples.Too Many Cooks Spoil The Broth: Five people editing one report proved too many cooks spoil the broth.
repaired_examples.To Pull Or Twist Out Of Shape: The heat pulled the plastic frame out of shape.
repaired_examples.All Good Things Must Come To An End: The holiday ended sadly, but all good things must come to an end.
repaired_examples.Barking Up The Wrong Tree: By blaming the peon, the officer was barking up the wrong tree.
repaired_examples.Back To The Drawing Board: The prototype failed, so the team went back to the drawing board.
repaired_examples.Blood Is Thicker Than Water: He supported his cousin in the dispute — blood is thicker than water.
repaired_examples.A Fool And His Money Are Easily Parted: He spent his prize on lottery tickets; a fool and his money are easily parted.
repaired_examples.The Squeaky Wheel Gets The Grease: She complained daily until her file moved — the squeaky wheel gets the grease.
repaired_examples.To Bite Off More Than You Can Chew: Running two businesses at once, he bit off more than he could chew.
repaired_examples.Birds Of A Feather Flock Together: The two idlers spent all day together; birds of a feather flock together.
repaired_examples.Every Cloud Has A Silver Lining: He lost the job but found a better one — every cloud has a silver lining.
repaired_examples.To Go Down Like A Lead Balloon: His joke went down like a lead balloon before the serious panel.
repaired_examples.To Get Up On The Wrong Side Of The Bed: He snapped at everyone; clearly he got up on the wrong side of the bed.
repaired_examples.To Put One Out Of Countenance: The sharp question put the speaker out of countenance.
repaired_examples.To Keep The Wolf From The Door: He took evening tuitions to keep the wolf from the door.
repaired_examples.To Put A Spoke In One's Wheel: His rival put a spoke in his wheel by delaying the paperwork.
repaired_examples.To Go Through Fire And Water: A mother will go through fire and water for her children.
repaired_examples.Live From Hand To Mouth: The family lived from hand to mouth after the factory closed.
repaired_examples.The Thin End Of The Wedge: Allowing one exception may be the thin end of the wedge.
repaired_examples.To Wrangle Over An Ass's Shadow: They wrangled over an ass's shadow instead of solving the real issue.
repaired_examples.To Have A Finger In Every Pie: The busy trustee has a finger in every pie in the town.
repaired_examples.To Hit The Nail On The Head: Her comment hit the nail on the head and ended the debate.
repaired_examples.To Have Something On The Brain: Ever since the result, he has the interview on the brain.
repaired_examples.To Have At One's Fingertips: A good teacher has the whole syllabus at her fingertips.
repaired_examples.Let Something Slip Through One's Fingers: He let a golden opportunity slip through his fingers.
repaired_examples.Get Up On The Wrong Side Of The Bed: She was grumpy all morning, having got up on the wrong side of the bed.
